skill.name: pptx-generator
skill.description: Create, edit, and inspect PowerPoint presentations using PptxGenJS, markitdown, and PPTX XML workflows. The skill supports new deck generation, template-based editing, slide structure planning, visual design guidance, and output QA.
skill.summary: Create and edit PowerPoint decks with structured slide generation, template workflows, and QA guidance.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found most are false positives from Markdown code examples, color hex values, XML placeholders, and documentation URLs. No prompt injection attempt or malicious exfiltration intent was found. The skill still carries medium operational risk because it instructs users to install dependencies, run generated JavaScript, and manipulate PPTX ZIP/XML files.

medium_findings.title: Generated JavaScript Execution Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to create slide modules, dynamically require local slide files, and run a Node compile script. This is expected for PptxGenJS deck generation, but generated JavaScript can execute arbitrary local code if the workspace or slide files are untrusted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dynamic require and node execution are explicitly documented, but they load predictable local slide files for a legitimate deck-building workflow. Risk depends on whether generated or user-provided slide modules are reviewed before execution.

medium_findings.title: Dependency Installation and Local Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The workflow asks users to run markitdown, Node, pip, and npm commands. These commands are part of the stated PowerPoint workflow, but they expand the local execution surface and depend on package trust.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible documentation, not hidden behavior. They are operationally risky because installing and executing external tooling can affect the host environment.

low_findings.title: PPTX ZIP and XML Filesystem Manipulation
low_findings.description: The editing workflow instructs users to unpack PPTX files, edit XML, write temporary files, and repack the archive. This is legitimate for template editing, but archive extraction should avoid unsafe paths and preserve the original file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence shows intended filesystem work on user-provided PPTX files, not malicious path traversal. The risk is implementation hygiene around archive handling and temporary paths.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs and Remote Image Examples
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs appear in metadata sources and PptxGenJS examples for images and hyperlinks. I did not find evidence that the skill exfiltrates data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are public documentation links or example asset URLs. They do not include credentials, payload construction, or instructions to send user data.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Keyword Matches Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: Many high-risk static hits are caused by hex colors, typography examples, XML ellipses, and layout text. These do not indicate weak cryptography, command and control behavior, or reconnaissance intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines show color palettes and XML formatting examples. The semantic context confirms they are presentation design references, not security-sensitive code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic Local Module Loading
dangerous_patterns.description: The compile script example loads slide files with a computed local path. This is useful for generated slide modules but should only run trusted files in the slides directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit and executable in the generated compile script. It is constrained to local slide filenames, so the risk is moderate rather than high.

dangerous_patterns.title: Global Package Installation Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The dependency section recommends global npm installation and pip installation. This is a common setup pattern, but global installs can affect the host and should use trusted package sources.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly user-visible setup instructions. The risk comes from package execution and global environment changes, not hidden malware behavior.

content.user_title: Create PowerPoint Decks from Content and Templates
content.value_statement: Building polished presentations by hand is slow and error-prone. This skill turns content or templates into structured PowerPoint decks with design and QA guidance.
actual_capabilities: Creates new PowerPoint decks with PptxGenJS slide modules.
Edits existing PPTX templates through XML-based workflows.
Extracts presentation text with markitdown for review and QA.
Provides slide type guidance for covers, agendas, content slides, dividers, and summaries.
Includes color palettes, typography guidance, spacing rules, and common PptxGenJS pitfalls.
Defines output folders for slide modules, images, previews, and final PPTX files.
limitations: Requires local installation of PptxGenJS and markitdown for full execution.
Generated JavaScript should be reviewed before running in sensitive workspaces.
Template editing depends on the structure and quality of the source PPTX file.
The skill provides workflow guidance, not a standalone hosted presentation service.

use_cases.title: Create an Executive Briefing Deck
use_cases.description: Turn a strategy memo or research summary into a structured PowerPoint deck with clear sections and polished visual hierarchy.

use_cases.title: Refresh a Client Template
use_cases.description: Reuse an existing PPTX template, replace placeholder content, remove unused slides, and preserve the client design system.

use_cases.title: Build Teaching Slides
use_cases.description: Convert lesson notes into a presentation with agenda slides, content slides, diagrams, summaries, and QA checks.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new presentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 6-slide PowerPoint deck about [topic] for [audience]. Include a cover, agenda, three content slides, and a summary.

prompt_templates.title: Turn Notes into Slides
prompt_templates.prompt: Use these notes to create a professional PowerPoint deck. Choose slide types, organize the content, and include QA steps before final output: [notes].

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit the provided PPTX template for [purpose]. Preserve the visual style, replace placeholders, remove unused slides, and save an updated deck.

prompt_templates.title: Generate and Verify a Branded Deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with design and validation requirements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a branded PowerPoint deck for [campaign]. Use the provided palette and fonts, generate previews, extract text for QA, and fix any issues found.

output_examples.input: A product launch brief with audience, goals, key features, timeline, and closing call to action.
output_examples.output: A polished launch deck with a cover slide, agenda, feature overview, timeline slide, benefits slide, and summary slide.

output_examples.input: An existing investor update template plus new quarterly metrics and team notes.
output_examples.output: An edited PPTX that preserves the template layout, updates metrics, removes unused placeholders, and passes text extraction review.

output_examples.input: Training notes for a one-hour workshop on customer support workflows.
output_examples.output: A structured training presentation with section dividers, process slides, comparison content, and a final recap slide.

best_practices: Review generated slide JavaScript before running the compile script.
Use a copy of any user-provided PPTX template and preserve the original file.
Run markitdown QA after generation or editing to catch missing text and placeholders.
anti_patterns: Do not run generated slide modules from an untrusted workspace.
Do not reuse the same slide layout for every content section.
Do not edit PPTX XML without updating related presentation and relationship files.

faq.question: Can this skill create a deck from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides PptxGenJS slide generation, slide type planning, design choices, previews, and final PPTX output.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing PowerPoint template?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes a template workflow using copied PPTX files, XML edits, slide mapping, cleanup, and repacking.

faq.question: Does it require extra software?
faq.answer: Yes. Full use requires tools such as PptxGenJS, Node.js, Python, and markitdown.

faq.question: Is it safe to run generated slide files?
faq.answer: Review generated JavaScript first, especially in sensitive workspaces. The compile step executes local slide modules.

faq.question: Can it read slide content for review?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow uses markitdown to extract text from PPTX files and verify output quality.

faq.question: Does it handle visual design guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes palettes, font recommendations, slide layout patterns, spacing rules, and PptxGenJS pitfalls.
